expectations.evidence: 4 observability shell scripts generated inside .harness/observability/: log.sh (18KB), metrics.sh (18KB), health.sh (15KB), trace.sh (21KB). Well exceeds the minimum of 2.

All 4 scripts contain substantial executable logic: case-based CLI dispatch, real grep/find/jq/sed pipelines querying source files, functional commands (cmd_recent, cmd_errors, cmd_telemetry, cmd_summary, etc.), health check functions with pass/warn/fail tracking, trace validation with jq-based JSON inspection, and Prometheus/OTLP metric extraction. No placeholder-only content.

All scripts set PROJECT_ROOT='/Users/josh_folder/Open-ClaudeCode' and reference actual project paths: src/utils/telemetry/ (instrumentation.ts, sessionTracing.ts, perfettoTracing.ts, bigqueryExporter.ts, events.ts), src/services/analytics/ (datadog.ts, sink.ts, firstPartyEventLogger.ts, config.ts, metadata.ts, growthbook.ts, sinkKillswitch.ts), package/package.json, rules/scripts/, and environment variables from the real codebase (CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY, OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER, etc.).

log.sh header explicitly documents O1 (Feedback), O2 (Persist), O3 (Queryable) coverage. The cmd_summary() function outputs a full 'Observability Level Assessment' section: O1 Feedback Level 3, O2 Persist Level 3, O3 Queryable Level 2, O4 Attribute Level 2. trace.sh header documents O4 (Attribute) and O2 (Persist). health.sh checks telemetry, analytics, and privacy subsystems covering all dimensions indirectly.
